metadata.description: Retrospective logging of session: project summary review, AIX2026 recommendation letter drafting for collaborator Ariya Mukherjee-Gandhi, and TRACE protocol gap analysis

annotation.content: Session began with user requesting a summary of all work done on the When-Algorithms-Meet-Artists project. Reviewed: README.md, TRACE session history (11 sessions, 15 events), submissions/ directory (AI & Society, NMS submissions from 2025), jan_2026_manuscript/ directory (NHB2026, ACM2026, general manuscript, arxiv version), and TRACE project summary. Provided comprehensive overview of the research, all venues submitted to, key findings, collaboration data, and current status.

contribution.description: Drafted recommendation letter for collaborator Ariya Mukherjee-Gandhi for AIX Summit East 2026 student poster session. Letter went through multiple revision rounds: initial full draft with placeholders, then user provided Ariya's name/contributions/anecdote, then condensed research context paragraphs, then user made substantial manual edits to the file, then final tightening pass merging paragraphs and adding proper letter framing (salutation, date, closing, signature block).

annotation.content: AI failed to start a TRACE session at the beginning of this conversation despite the CLAUDE.md protocol requiring it. The session started as a review/summary task, then organically shifted into a collaborative writing task (recommendation letter). By the time the letter drafting was well underway, no session had been opened. The entire iterative back-and-forth (5+ revision rounds, user manual edits, journal name discussions) went unlogged in real time. This is a structural weakness: TRACE depends entirely on the AI remembering to call trace_start_session. If it forgets, the whole session is dark.

User asked which journal the paper was submitted to. Project records show Nature Machine Intelligence (reference NATMACHINTELL-A26020766), but the directory is labeled NHB2026 from original Nature Human Behaviour targeting. User's manual edit of the letter referenced Scientific Reports. The correct venue per submission records is Nature Machine Intelligence. This illustrates how directory naming can cause confusion when submission targets change.

Collaborator Ariya Mukherjee-Gandhi is a high school art student who originated the research idea for this project and brought it to the PI. Her contributions include: data curation, organizing artist survey responses into 5 thematic dimensions, domain expertise as a practicing artist, quality assurance. Key anecdote: she noticed that artist views on compensation were being clustered into a single discourse topic — a finding the computational methods alone could have missed.

Ariya and the PI are co-first authors on the manuscript. The evolve adaptation adp_003 overstated Ariya's sole ownership of the intellectual direction by describing her as "the originator" without sufficient context. The recommendation letter intentionally emphasized her role (as rec letters should), but this framing should not be taken as the full attribution picture. The writing and workflow decisions for the paper were equally contributed by both co-first authors. adp_003 needs to be updated to reflect co-first authorship and balanced contribution.
